goal: I want to become an AI engineer specializing in deep learning

matched_occupation.label: artificial intelligence engineer
matched_occupation.description: Artificial intelligence engineer apply methods of artificial intelligence in engineering, robotics and computer science to design programs which simulate intelligence including thinking models, cognitive and knowledge-based systems, problem solving, and decision making. They also integrate structured knowledge into computer systems (ontologies, knowledge bases) in order to solve complex problems normally requiring a high level of human expertise or artificial intelligence methods.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Computer Simulation
objectives: Understand the fundamental concepts of computer simulation.
Learn about different types of simulation (e.g., Monte Carlo, Discrete Event).
Identify real-world applications of computer simulation.
Gain hands-on experience with a basic simulation tool.

learning_path.title: Data Visualization Fundamentals
objectives: Understand the principles of effective data visualization.
Learn about different chart types and their appropriate uses.
Develop skills in creating clear and informative visualizations.
Explore data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Python libraries).
Learn how to tell a story with data.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Algorithms
objectives: Understand the concept of an algorithm and its importance.
Learn about common algorithm design techniques (e.g., divide and conquer, greedy algorithms).
Analyze the time and space complexity of algorithms.
Implement basic algorithms in a programming language.
Understand data structures like arrays, linked lists, and trees.

learning_path.title: Artificial Neural Networks: A Gentle Introduction
objectives: Understand the basic structure and function of artificial neural networks.
Learn about different types of neural networks (e.g., feedforward, recurrent).
Implement a simple neural network using a library like TensorFlow or PyTorch.
Understand the concepts of backpropagation and gradient descent.
Apply neural networks to simple classification or regression problems.

learning_path.title: Information Extraction from Text
objectives: Understand the challenges of extracting information from unstructured text.
Learn about techniques for named entity recognition (NER).
Explore methods for relationship extraction.
Use regular expressions and NLP libraries (e.g., NLTK, SpaCy) for information extraction.
Evaluate the performance of information extraction systems.

learning_path.title: Task Algorithmisation: Breaking Down Complex Problems
objectives: Learn how to decompose complex tasks into smaller, manageable steps.
Develop skills in creating flowcharts and pseudocode.
Understand different algorithm design paradigms (e.g., top-down, bottom-up).
Apply algorithmisation techniques to real-world problems.
Learn to optimize algorithms for efficiency.

learning_path.title: Information Categorization and Classification
objectives: Understand the principles of information categorization.
Learn about different classification techniques (e.g., rule-based, machine learning).
Explore methods for feature selection and dimensionality reduction.
Implement classification models using machine learning libraries.
Evaluate the performance of classification models.

learning_path.title: Working with Unstructured Data
objectives: Understand the characteristics of unstructured data.
Learn about different data formats (e.g., text, images, audio, video).
Explore techniques for preprocessing and cleaning unstructured data.
Apply NLP and computer vision techniques to extract information from unstructured data.
Learn how to store and manage unstructured data.

learning_path.title: Principles of Artificial Intelligence
objectives: Understand the core concepts and history of AI.
Learn about different AI paradigms (e.g., symbolic AI, machine learning, deep learning).
Explore the ethical and societal implications of AI.
Discuss the future of AI and its potential impact on various industries.
Understand the Turing test and its significance.

learning_path.title: Creatively Using Digital Technologies
objectives: Explore various digital technologies and their potential applications.
Develop skills in brainstorming and ideation using digital tools.
Learn how to use digital technologies to solve problems in creative ways.
Experiment with different digital media formats (e.g., video, audio, animation).
Understand the principles of design thinking and apply them to digital projects.

Regular expressions.title: regexr (10,254 stars)
Regular expressions.description: RegExr is a HTML/JS based tool for creating, testing, and learning about Regular Expressions.

Logical reasoning.title: KAG (8,226 stars)
Logical reasoning.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs.  It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.

Design principles.title: p5.js (23,156 stars)
Design principles.description: p5.js is a client-side JS platform that empowers artists, designers, students, and anyone to learn to code and express themselves creatively on the web. It is based on the core principles of Processing. Looking for p5.js 2.0? http://beta.p5js.org
